verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (HSP60) is a mitochondrial matrix chaperone canonically resident in the mitochondrial inner compartment. However, it has documented surface display in multiple non-baseline contexts: (1) cancer-cell ecto-presentation — HSPD1 is widely reported on the outer leaflet of tumor cells and is a target of CAR-T and antibody programs in oncology; (2) cell-death-induced externalization — apoptosis and immunogenic cell death move HSPD1 to the outer PM face as a damage-associated molecular pattern (DAMP); (3) stress-induced translocation — heat shock and metabolic stress induce HSPD1 surface exposure in some cell types. The cancer-cell ecto-pool and DAMPs-induced display are well-documented, qualifying HSPD1 as contextual/cell_state_induced rather than no.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Baseline compartment is unambiguous (mitochondrial matrix), but magnitude and generalizability of cancer/stress-induced surface display across cell types and tumor models is not fully quantified; therapeutic targeting (CAR-T, antibody) confirms the ecto-pool is biologically real and therapeutically accessible in oncology contexts, but the frequency and stability of surface residence in non-cancer cells under stress remain partially characterized.
